overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Showcase landing page with positioning, quick integration guidance, and the next content stages.

mission.title: Mission
mission.description: Explains why the new showcase exists and what runtime/data-driven foundation is already in place.
bullets: Positions the new showcase as the canonical docs-shell app on top of `@feugene/granularity`.
Key runtime metrics: public registry, built-in themes and foundation tokens.
The top hero shows right away that the showcase runs on the new shell architecture, not on legacy.

quick-start.title: Quick start
quick-start.description: Shows the recommended integration paths and the package's real code entrypoints.
bullets: Quick start through the root API and ready-made CSS exports.
Granular component imports for a precise bundle.
Production path through the `UnoCSS` preset when the app already builds styles on its side.

delivery-checklist.title: Delivery checklist
delivery-checklist.description: Captures what the current plan stages already confirm and which content comes next.
bullets: The showcase already runs, builds and uses the package as its primary UI toolkit.
Foundations now begins to cover onboarding guides, narrative docs and quick-start snippets.
The next major step after foundations is live entity pages for components, directives and utilities.

roadmap.title: Roadmap
roadmap.description: Shows the nearest product stages of filling the showcase after foundations.
bullets: First — scenario pages for {componentTitles}.
Then — interactive scenarios for {directiveTitles}.
Utility coverage will start with {utilityTitles}.

foundations.title: Foundations
foundations.description: Core onboarding docs: installation paths, styling layers, themes, tokens, UnoCSS, and localization.

installation.title: Installation paths
installation.description: The setup progression for `presetGranular` (the node variant `presetGranularNode`) — the only supported installation method.
bullets: Quick-start snippets right on the foundations page: from a basic config to the advanced `granularContent`.
Narrative guidance is wired in from `packages/granularity/docs/installation.md`.
One screen shows step by step how to grow the `components`, `themes`, `layer` and `granularContent` options.

styling.title: Styling, themes and tokens
styling.description: Shows the boundaries between foundation layers, theme files and design tokens.
bullets: Real excerpts from `styling.md`, `tokens.css`, `light.css` and `dark.css` are wired in.
There is a clear split between `tokens`, the theme layer and component-level CSS.
The runtime contract is shown through `initThemeEarly()` and `useTheme()`.

unocss.title: UnoCSS and localization
unocss.description: Covers the production preset path and the package's i18n contract for the host app.
bullets: `presetGranularNode` from `@feugene/unocss-preset-granular/node` is the only supported way to wire it up.
The localization narrative doc with the package-level i18n entrypoint is wired in.
Foundations now serves as a real onboarding base rather than a placeholder section.

components.title: Components
components.description: A component catalog with search, grouping, and a dedicated page for each public package component.

catalog.title: Component catalog
catalog.description: This will hold the index page with all public components and quick grouping by category.
bullets: The source of truth is `packages/granularity/src/granular-provider/shared.ts`, currently covering {componentCount} component entities.
Component navigation should be instant and search-friendly.
Every component will get its own route and its own page.

live-demos.title: Live demos
live-demos.description: Each component page will reveal real usage scenarios rather than just a dump of every prop.
bullets: The first hand-authored demo scenarios are already reserved for {componentTitles}.
Dedicated coverage of states, composition cases and complex scenarios.
Example code and live preview will live side by side.

api-sync.title: Generated API
api-sync.description: API tables should be assembled from the package source and never drift from the real components.
bullets: Props, slots, events and expose/methods are already represented in a unified pending API format.
Fallback metadata is used only where autogeneration is not enough.
Autogeneration is tied to hand-authored demo descriptions.

directives.title: Directives
directives.description: A section for public directives and `createLoading` with real interactive scenarios.

public-api.title: Covered directives
public-api.description: The showcase will include all public directives from the package exports, not just the most obvious cases.
bullets: {directiveList} are already lifted from the public exports.
Dedicated docs for `createLoading` next to `vLoading`.
Recording caveats, parameters and expected behavior.

interactive-cases.title: Interactive behavior
interactive-cases.description: Users should see a directive in action, not just copy a usage snippet.
bullets: Priority scenarios are already highlighted for {directiveTitles}.
Explanations of handling edge cases and UX constraints.
Ready-made examples for real integration.

integration-notes.title: Integration notes
integration-notes.description: Each directive will get explicit notes on setup, parameters and compatibility with components.
bullets: Binding/value signatures and typical input data.
Combinations with design-system components.
Constraints and caveats for embedded/SSR-like scenarios where needed.

composables.title: Composables
composables.description: Pages for `useTheme` and `useToast` with runnable integration recipes.

use-theme.title: `useTheme` scenarios
use-theme.description: The showcase already uses `useTheme()` for the global toggle, and this page will explore the composable more deeply.
bullets: Initialization and early theme application.
Persist mode and behavior in the interface.
Wiring the theme toggle to showcase components.

use-toast.title: `useToast` scenarios
use-toast.description: Interactive push/dismiss/clear examples and notification variants will appear here.
bullets: The current composables registry already contains {composableList}.
Clearing the queue and managing the toast message lifecycle.
Explanations of expected usage and integration patterns.

integration-recipes.title: Integration recipes
integration-recipes.description: Each composable will be shaped as a ready integration recipe rather than a dry list of return values.
bullets: Parameters, return values and code snippets.
Practical integration patterns into the app shell and feature pages.
Links to the runtime theme, overlay scenarios and notifications.

utilities.title: Utilities
utilities.description: A section for package-level utilities starting with `fileValidation` and applied usage scenarios.

file-validation.title: `fileValidation`
file-validation.description: A dedicated page will show the key validators and the format of the returned results.
bullets: {utilityCount} utility entities of the file validation layer are already collected from the public exports.
Practical validation errors and constraints.
Comparing the use case without UI and together with `GrFileUpload`.

integration.title: Utility integration
integration.description: Utilities should explain not only the API but also the real way to apply them in a product.
bullets: Priority integration points are already highlighted for {utilityTitles}.
Integration examples into forms and file upload flows.
Documenting errors, constraints and edge cases.

future-coverage.title: Future coverage
future-coverage.description: The section will stay extensible to safely include new package-level helpers as the package grows.
bullets: Preparing the structure for new utilities.
A single format for narrative notes and runnable examples.
Consistency with search and generated metadata in future stages.

integration.title: Integration
integration.description: Two additional integration options: the runtime adapter `@feugene/granularity/vue` (`createGranularity`) and build-time auto-import via `@feugene/unplugin-granularity` (a `unplugin-vue-components` resolver).

vue-plugin.title: The `createGranularity` Vue plugin
vue-plugin.description: A lightweight runtime adapter from `@feugene/granularity/vue`: it registers components, directives, `provide` keys and `globalProperties` without losing tree-shaking — the module itself imports no components.
bullets: `createGranularity({ components, directives, provides, globalProperties })` returns a standard Vue plugin for `app.use(...)`.
Supports three component forms: a plain SFC, an object with `install`, and an explicit `{ name, component }`.
The main use case is directives, `provide`, `globalProperties`, SSR/Nuxt plugins and bundler-less environments.
Typical cases: global directives (`v-hotkey`, `v-autofocus`), `provide` for i18n/theme, `globalProperties` for the Options API.

unplugin.title: Auto-import with `@feugene/unplugin-granularity`
unplugin.description: A separate build-time resolver package for `unplugin-vue-components`: it injects static `import`s for the package's granular subpath exports directly into SFCs.
bullets: Write `<GrButton />` and `v-hotkey` in a template — the resolver adds the needed imports and pulls in component-level CSS.
Options: `prefix`, `importStyle`, `directives`, `exclude`; directives are resolved through an explicit whitelist.
Recommended for production apps: perfect tree-shaking and no manual `import`s.
Typical setup: the resolver together with `presetGranularNode` from `@feugene/unocss-preset-granular/node` to auto-import components in templates.

when-to-use.title: How to choose between the approaches
when-to-use.description: The tools are orthogonal and typically used together: the resolver covers templates, the plugin covers directives outside templates, `provide` and globals.
bullets: `@feugene/unplugin-granularity` is build-time and covers only what actually appears in `<template>`.
`@feugene/granularity/vue` is runtime and covers everything else: directives in `render()`/JSX, i18n/theme via `provide`, the Options API via `globalProperties`.
SSR / tests / environments without `unplugin-vue-components` — only through `createGranularity`.
Installing the package's CSS part is done exclusively through `presetGranularNode` — see Foundations / Installation paths.

componentsPage.heroDescription: Search components by name and group, then jump to the detail page without extra noise.
componentsPage.emptyState: No components matched the current query. Try a component name (`GrButton`) or a group (`forms`, `overlays`).

packageCatalog.heroDescription: Here you can quickly find the required entity and open its detail page.
packageCatalog.emptyState: Nothing matched the current query. Try searching by export name or group.

foundationsPage.heroTitle: Foundations brings installation, themes, and core setup together in one clear section.
foundationsPage.heroDescription: Only the steps that matter for getting started are collected here: installation, style wiring, and key integration notes.
foundationsPage.quickStartDescription: Choose the integration path that fits your app: from a simple import to an `UnoCSS` preset.
foundationsPage.currentTokenRegistryDescription: This table is built from the current `packages/granularity/src/styles/tokens.css`, so it reflects the actual foundation tokens in the package.
foundationsPage.currentThemeRegistryDescription: The table shows semantic theme tokens from the current `light.css` and `dark.css` files.

liveExamples.title: Live examples
liveExamples.descriptionComponent: Short usage scenarios showing the component inside a real interface.
liveExamples.descriptionPackage: Isolated scenarios help you quickly understand how to use the entity in an interface.
liveExamples.descriptionGeneric: Illustrative usage scenarios without unnecessary explanations.

api.title: API
api.descriptionPackage: Everything about parameters and the usage contract gathered in one place.

implementationNotes.title: Implementation notes
implementationNotes.description: Additional notes on accessibility, dependencies, and related materials.

accessibility.componentTheme: Verify on-screen states for light and dark themes.

search.placeholder: For example: GrButton, file validation, useTheme

overviewPage.tagline: A design-system showcase for Vue 3: ready-made components, clear styling layers, granular imports and clean integration with `UnoCSS`.
overviewPage.whyHeading: Everything you need so the design system helps the team instead of slowing it down

0.title: Builds product interfaces faster
0.description: Ready-made components, tokens and themes take routine off the team and speed up shipping screens.

1.title: Adopts without a rigid migration path
1.description: Start with a simple import, then move to granular imports and a precise CSS setup as the product grows.

2.title: Gives neat control over styles and bundle
2.description: Clear styling layers, subpath exports and UnoCSS integration help you keep things manageable.

3.title: Fits both a quick start and mature infrastructure
3.description: One package covers scenarios from a few ready components to dependency-aware integration.

overviewPage.quickStartSubtitle: Five documented integration scenarios: from a root import to UnoCSS presets.
overviewPage.installSummaryText: Below is a short excerpt from the docs that helps you quickly pick an integration approach.
